{{神社| 名称=塩竈神社| 所在地=和歌山県和歌山市和歌浦中3-4-25| 祭神=塩槌翁尊| 社格=不詳| 創建=[[1917年]]| 画像=| 例祭=[[9月16日]]}} '''塩竈神社'''（しおがまじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]][[和歌浦]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。
== 由緒 == [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、塩竈神社は[[玉津島神社]]の[[祓所]]から神社になった。海産物、安産の神様として信仰されてきた神社で、[[御神体]]の[[塩槌翁尊]]は'''輿の窟'''という岩穴に鎮座する。
== 祭礼 == *[[9月16日]] - 例祭
== 境外社 == *[[玉津島神社]]

== 由緒 == [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、塩竈神社は[[玉津島神社]]の[[祓所]]から神社になった。海産物、安産の神様として信仰されてきた神社で、[[御神体]]の[[塩槌翁尊]]は'''輿の窟'''という岩穴に鎮座する。

{{神社| 名称=塩竈神社| 所在地=和歌山県和歌山市和歌浦中3-4-25| 祭神=塩槌翁尊| 社格=不詳| 創建=[[1917年]]| 画像=| 例祭=[[9月16日]]}} '''塩竈神社'''（しおがまじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]][[和歌浦]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。
== 由緒 == [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、塩竈神社は[[玉津島神社]]の[[祓所]]から神社になった。海産物、安産の神様として信仰されてきた神社で、[[御神体]]の[[塩槌翁尊]]は'''輿の窟'''という岩穴に鎮座する。
== 祭礼 == *[[9月16日]] - 例祭

{{DEFAULTSORT:しおかましんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:和歌山市の神社]]

{{神社| 名称=塩竈神社| 所在地=和歌山県和歌山市和歌浦中3-4-25| 祭神=塩槌翁尊| 社格=不詳| 創建=[[1917年]]| 画像=| 例祭=[[9月16日]]}} '''塩竈神社'''（しおがまじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]][[和歌浦]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。
== 由緒 == [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、塩竈神社は[[玉津島神社]]の[[祓所]]から神社になった。海産物、安産の神様として信仰されてきた神社で、[[御神体]]の[[塩槌翁尊]]は'''輿の窟'''という岩穴に鎮座する。
結晶片岩で出来た鏡山の南面に位置する。岩肌は曝れた木理のような観を呈することから伽羅岩と呼ばれ、岩と松の組み合わさった風景が玉津島の原風景を今に伝える。祠は、海風により自然に出来た洞窟である。祠の中には小さな拝殿が造られている。元は玉津島神社の抜所で、輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた。輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と言われるのは、かつて浜降り神事の際に神輿が奉置される場所であったからである。浜降りとは、毎年9月16日に高野山の地主神である天野丹生都比売神社の神輿が、紀ノ川沿いをはるばる玉津嶋神社まで渡御し、翌日日前宮へと御行してゆく神事をいい、神輿が玉津嶋神社で一晩奉置されるところが興ノ窟であった。浜降り神事はその起源を古代にまで遡ることができると考えられるが、鎌倉時代に一時中断された時期があり、文保2年（1318）に再開されたことが記録に残されている。その後戦国期に途絶え、近世には天野社の鳥居外から玉津嶋神社を遥拝するなどの神事になっている。ただ、この窟に対する信仰は、浜降り神事に限られたものではなく、江戸時代後期には、「しおかま」の名で信仰の対象になっていた。江戸時代に「一に権現、二に玉津島、三に下り松、四に塩竃よ」と歌われ、塩田の塩を焼く釜からこの名が付けられたという。また、古くから安産の守神として祀られてきたことが考えられる。現在も安産の守護として、人々に親しまれている。
神社近くの小高い丘には、干潟を望むかのように山部赤人の有名な歌碑が建っている。
== 祭礼 == *[[9月16日]] - 例祭
== 境外社 == *[[玉津島神社]]
== 文化財 == *[[和歌山県指定文化財 名勝・史跡和歌の浦 ]]
== 交通 == *[[和歌山バス]]『玉津島神社前』下車すぐ
== 関連項目 == *[[玉津島神社]] *[[不老橋]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しおかましんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:和歌山市の神社]]

revision.comment: 「 境外社」トル（ 玉津島神社の境外社が塩竈神社なので）

{{神社| 名称=塩竈神社| 所在地=和歌山県和歌山市和歌浦中3-4-25| 祭神=塩槌翁尊| 社格=不詳| 創建=[[1917年]]| 画像=[[File:Shiogama-jinja (Wakayama, Wakayama).jpg|220px]]| 例祭=[[9月16日]]}} '''塩竈神社'''（しおがまじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]][[和歌浦]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。
== 由緒 == [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、塩竈神社は[[玉津島神社]]の[[祓所]]から神社になった。海産物、安産の神様として信仰されてきた神社で、[[御神体]]の[[塩槌翁尊]]は'''輿の窟'''という岩穴に鎮座する。
結晶片岩で出来た鏡山の南面に位置する。岩肌は曝れた木理のような観を呈することから伽羅岩と呼ばれ、岩と松の組み合わさった風景が玉津島の原風景を今に伝える。祠は、海風により自然に出来た洞窟である。祠の中には小さな拝殿が造られている。元は玉津島神社の抜所で、輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた。輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた理由は、かつて浜降り神事の際に神輿が奉置される場所であったからである。浜降りとは、毎年9月16日に高野山の地主神である天野丹生都比売神社の神輿が、紀ノ川沿いをはるばる玉津嶋神社まで渡御し、翌日に日前宮へと御行してゆく神事をいい、神輿が玉津嶋神社で一晩奉置されるところが興ノ窟であった。浜降り神事はその起源を古代にまで遡ることができると考えられるが、鎌倉時代に一時中断された時期があり、文保2年（1318）に再開されたことが記録に残されている。その後戦国期に途絶え、近世には天野社の鳥居外から玉津嶋神社を遥拝するなどの神事になっている。ただ、この窟に対する信仰は、浜降り神事に限られたものではなく、江戸時代後期には、「しおかま」の名で信仰の対象になっていた。江戸時代の和歌山では「一に権現（[[紀州東照宮]]）、二に玉津島、三に下り松、四に塩竃よ」と歌われ、塩田の塩を焼く釜からこの名が付けられたという。また、古くから安産の守神として祀られてきたことが考えられる。現在も安産の守護として、人々に親しまれている。
神社近くの小高い丘には、干潟を望むかのように山部赤人の有名な歌碑が建っている。
== 祭礼 == *[[9月16日]] - 例祭
== 文化財 == *[[和歌の浦|和歌山県指定文化財 名勝・史跡和歌の浦]]
== 交通 == *[[和歌山バス]]『玉津島神社前』下車すぐ
== 関連項目 == *[[玉津島神社]] *[[不老橋]]

{{神社| 名称=塩竈神社| 所在地=[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]和歌浦中3-4-25| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|11|13.5|N|135|10|22.2|E|region:JP}}| 祭神=塩槌翁尊| 社格=不詳| 創建=[[1917年]]| 画像=[[File:Shiogama-jinja Wakanoura Wakayama Japan02s3.jpg|220px]]| 例祭=[[4月13日]]}} '''塩竈神社'''（しおがまじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]][[和歌浦]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。
== 由緒 == [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、塩竈神社は[[玉津島神社]]の[[祓所]]から神社になった。海産物、安産の神として信仰されてきた神社で、[[神体]]の[[塩槌翁尊]]は'''輿の窟'''という岩穴に鎮座する。
結晶片岩でできた鏡山の南面に位置する。岩肌は曝れた木理のような観を呈することから伽羅岩と呼ばれ、岩と松の組み合わさった風景が玉津島の原風景を今に伝える。祠は、海風により自然に形成された洞窟である。祠の中には小さな拝殿が造られている。元は玉津島神社の抜所で、輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた。輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた理由は、かつて浜降り神事の際に神輿が奉置される場所であったからである。浜降りとは、毎年9月16日に高野山の地主神である天野[[丹生都比売神社]]の神輿が、紀ノ川沿いをはるばる玉津嶋神社まで渡御し、翌日に日前宮へと御行してゆく神事をいい、神輿が玉津嶋神社で一晩奉置されるところが興ノ窟であった。浜降り神事はその起源を古代にまで遡ることができると考えられるが、鎌倉時代に一時中断された時期があり、文保2年（1318）に再開されたことが記録に残されている。その後戦国期に途絶え、近世には天野社の鳥居外から玉津嶋神社を遥拝するなどの神事になっている。ただ、この窟に対する信仰は、浜降り神事に限られたものではなく、江戸時代後期には、「しおかま」の名で信仰の対象になっていた。江戸時代の和歌山では「一に権現（[[紀州東照宮]]）、二に玉津島、三に下り松、四に塩竃よ」と歌われ、塩田の塩を焼く釜からこの名が付けられたという。また、古くから安産の守神として祀られてきたことが考えられる。現在も安産の守護として、人々に親しまれている。
神社近くの小高い丘には、干潟を望むかのように山部赤人の有名な歌碑<ref>平成24年6月に、歌碑とそれに隣接する、樹齢数百年の大木「和合の松」が倒壊した（「「和合の松」シロアリ被害で倒木」MSN産経ニュース、平成24年6月24日掲載）。現在、老松の再生を望む声もある。</ref>が建っている。
== 祭礼 == *[[4月13日]] - 例祭
== 文化財 == *[[和歌浦|和歌山県指定文化財 名勝・史跡和歌の浦]]
== 交通 == *[[和歌山バス]]『玉津島神社前』下車すぐ

{{神社| 名称=塩竈神社| 所在地=[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]和歌浦中3-4-25| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|11|13.5|N|135|10|22.2|E|region:JP}}| 祭神=塩槌翁尊| 社格=不詳| 創建=[[1917年]]| 画像=[[File:Shiogama-jinja Wakanoura Wakayama Japan02s3.jpg|220px]]| 例祭=[[4月13日]]}} '''塩竈神社'''（しおがまじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]][[和歌浦]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。
== 由緒 == [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、塩竈神社は[[玉津島神社]]の[[祓所]]から神社になった。海産物、安産の神として信仰されてきた神社で、[[神体]]の[[シオツチノオジ|塩槌翁尊]]は'''輿の窟'''という岩穴に鎮座する。
結晶片岩でできた鏡山の南面に位置する。岩肌は曝れた木理のような観を呈することから伽羅岩と呼ばれ、岩と松の組み合わさった風景が玉津島の原風景を今に伝える。祠は、海風により自然に形成された洞窟である。祠の中には小さな拝殿が造られている。元は玉津島神社の抜所で、輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた。輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた理由は、かつて浜降り神事の際に神輿が奉置される場所であったからである。浜降りとは、毎年9月16日に高野山の地主神である天野[[丹生都比売神社]]の神輿が、紀ノ川沿いをはるばる玉津嶋神社まで渡御し、翌日に日前宮へと御行してゆく神事をいい、神輿が玉津嶋神社で一晩奉置されるところが興ノ窟であった。浜降り神事はその起源を古代にまで遡ることができると考えられるが、鎌倉時代に一時中断された時期があり、文保2年（1318）に再開されたことが記録に残されている。その後戦国期に途絶え、近世には天野社の鳥居外から玉津嶋神社を遥拝するなどの神事になっている。ただ、この窟に対する信仰は、浜降り神事に限られたものではなく、江戸時代後期には、「しおかま」の名で信仰の対象になっていた。江戸時代の和歌山では「一に権現（[[紀州東照宮]]）、二に玉津島、三に下り松、四に塩竃よ」と歌われ、塩田の塩を焼く釜からこの名が付けられたという。また、古くから安産の守神として祀られてきたことが考えられる。現在も安産の守護として、人々に親しまれている。
神社近くの小高い丘には、干潟を望むかのように山部赤人の有名な歌碑<ref>平成24年6月に、歌碑とそれに隣接する、樹齢数百年の大木「和合の松」が倒壊した（「「和合の松」シロアリ被害で倒木」MSN産経ニュース、平成24年6月24日掲載）。現在、老松の再生を望む声もある。</ref>が建っている。
== 祭礼 == *[[4月13日]] - 例祭

{{神社| 名称=塩竈神社| 所在地=[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]和歌浦中3-4-25| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|11|13.5|N|135|10|22.2|E|region:JP-30_scale:10000|display=inline,title}}| 祭神=塩槌翁尊| 社格=不詳| 創建=[[1917年]]| 画像=[[File:Shiogama-jinja Wakanoura Wakayama Japan02s3.jpg|220px]]| 例祭=[[9月16日]]}} '''塩竈神社'''（しおがまじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]][[和歌浦]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。
== 由緒 == [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、塩竈神社は[[玉津島神社]]の[[祓所]]から神社になった。海産物、安産の神として信仰されてきた神社で、[[神体]]の[[シオツチノオジ|塩槌翁尊]]は'''輿の窟'''という岩穴に鎮座する。
結晶片岩でできた鏡山の南面に位置する。岩肌は曝れた木理のような観を呈することから伽羅岩と呼ばれ、岩と松の組み合わさった風景が玉津島の原風景を今に伝える。祠は、海風により自然に形成された洞窟である。祠の中には小さな拝殿が造られている。元は玉津島神社の抜所で、輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた。輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた理由は、かつて浜降り神事の際に神輿が奉置される場所であったからである。浜降りとは、毎年9月16日に高野山の地主神である天野[[丹生都比売神社]]の神輿が、紀ノ川沿いをはるばる玉津嶋神社まで渡御し、翌日に日前宮へと御行してゆく神事をいい、神輿が玉津嶋神社で一晩奉置されるところが興ノ窟であった。浜降り神事はその起源を古代にまで遡ることができると考えられるが、鎌倉時代に一時中断された時期があり、文保2年（1318）に再開されたことが記録に残されている。その後戦国期に途絶え、近世には天野社の鳥居外から玉津嶋神社を遥拝するなどの神事になっている。ただ、この窟に対する信仰は、浜降り神事に限られたものではなく、江戸時代後期には、「しおかま」の名で信仰の対象になっていた。江戸時代の和歌山では「一に権現（[[紀州東照宮]]）、二に玉津島、三に下り松、四に塩竃よ」と歌われ、塩田の塩を焼く釜からこの名が付けられたという。また、古くから安産の守神として祀られてきたことが考えられる。現在も安産の守護として、人々に親しまれている。
神社近くの小高い丘には、干潟を望むかのように山部赤人の有名な歌碑<ref>平成24年6月に、歌碑とそれに隣接する、樹齢数百年の大木「和合の松」が倒壊した（「「和合の松」シロアリ被害で倒木」MSN産経ニュース、平成24年6月24日掲載）。現在、老松の再生を望む声もある。</ref>が建っている。
== 祭礼 == *[[9月16日]] - 例祭

{{神社| 名称=鹽竈神社| 所在地=[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]和歌浦中3-4-25| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|11|13.5|N|135|10|22.2|E|region:JP-30_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=塩竈神社 (和歌山市)}}| 祭神=塩槌翁尊| 社格=不詳| 創建=[[1917年]]| 画像=[[File:Shiogama-jinja Wakanoura Wakayama Japan02s3.jpg|220px]]| 例祭=[[9月16日]]}} '''鹽竈神社'''（しおがまじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]][[和歌浦]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。
== 由緒 == [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、鹽竈神社は[[玉津島神社]]の[[祓所]]から神社になった。海産物、安産の神として信仰されてきた神社で、[[神体]]の[[シオツチノオジ|塩槌翁尊]]は'''輿の窟'''という岩穴に鎮座する。
結晶片岩でできた鏡山の南面に位置する。岩肌は曝れた木理のような観を呈することから伽羅岩と呼ばれ、岩と松の組み合わさった風景が玉津島の原風景を今に伝える。祠は、海風により自然に形成された洞窟である。祠の中には小さな拝殿が造られている。元は玉津島神社の抜所で、輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた。輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた理由は、かつて浜降り神事の際に神輿が奉置される場所であったからである。浜降りとは、毎年9月16日に高野山の地主神である天野[[丹生都比売神社]]の神輿が、紀ノ川沿いをはるばる玉津嶋神社まで渡御し、翌日に日前宮へと御行してゆく神事をいい、神輿が玉津嶋神社で一晩奉置されるところが興ノ窟であった。浜降り神事はその起源を古代にまで遡ることができると考えられるが、鎌倉時代に一時中断された時期があり、文保2年（1318）に再開されたことが記録に残されている。その後戦国期に途絶え、近世には天野社の鳥居外から玉津嶋神社を遥拝するなどの神事になっている。ただ、この窟に対する信仰は、浜降り神事に限られたものではなく、江戸時代後期には、「しおかま」の名で信仰の対象になっていた。江戸時代の和歌山では「一に権現（[[紀州東照宮]]）、二に玉津島、三に下り松、四に塩竃よ」と歌われ、塩田の塩を焼く釜からこの名が付けられたという。また、古くから安産の守神として祀られてきたことが考えられる。現在も安産の守護として、人々に親しまれている。
神社近くの小高い丘には、干潟を望むかのように山部赤人の有名な歌碑<ref>平成24年6月に、歌碑とそれに隣接する、樹齢数百年の大木「和合の松」が倒壊した（「「和合の松」シロアリ被害で倒木」MSN産経ニュース、平成24年6月24日掲載）。現在、老松の再生を望む声もある。</ref>が建っている。
== 祭礼 == *[[9月16日]] - 例祭
== 文化財 == *[[和歌浦|和歌山県指定文化財 名勝・史跡和歌の浦]]
== 交通 == *[[和歌山バス]]『玉津島神社前』下車すぐ
== 脚注 == <references />

{{神社 |名称=塩竈神社 |所在地=[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]]和歌浦中3-4-25 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|11|13.5|N|135|10|22.2|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=塩竈神社}} |祭神=塩槌翁尊 |社格=不詳 |創建=[[1917年]] |画像=[[File:Shiogama-jinja Wakanoura Wakayama Japan02s3.jpg|220px]] |例祭=[[9月16日]] }} '''塩竈神社'''（しおがまじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]][[和歌浦]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。
== 由緒 == [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、鹽竈神社は[[玉津島神社]]の[[祓所]]から神社になった。海産物、安産の神として信仰されてきた神社で、[[神体]]の[[シオツチノオジ|塩槌翁尊]]は'''輿の窟'''という岩穴に鎮座する。
結晶片岩でできた鏡山の南面に位置する。岩肌は曝れた木理のような観を呈することから伽羅岩と呼ばれ、岩と松の組み合わさった風景が玉津島の原風景を今に伝える。祠は、海風により自然に形成された洞窟である。祠の中には小さな拝殿が造られている。元は玉津島神社の抜所で、輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた。輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた理由は、かつて浜降り神事の際に神輿が奉置される場所であったからである。浜降りとは、毎年9月16日に高野山の地主神である天野[[丹生都比売神社]]の神輿が、紀ノ川沿いをはるばる玉津嶋神社まで渡御し、翌日に日前宮へと御行してゆく神事をいい、神輿が玉津嶋神社で一晩奉置されるところが興ノ窟であった。浜降り神事はその起源を古代にまで遡ることができると考えられるが、鎌倉時代に一時中断された時期があり、文保2年（[[1318年]]）に再開されたことが記録に残されている。その後戦国期に途絶え、近世には天野社の鳥居外から玉津嶋神社を遥拝するなどの神事になっている。ただ、この窟に対する信仰は、浜降り神事に限られたものではなく、江戸時代後期には、「しおかま」の名で信仰の対象になっていた。江戸時代の和歌山では「一に権現（[[紀州東照宮]]）、二に玉津島、三に下り松、四に塩竃よ」と歌われ、塩田の塩を焼く釜からこの名が付けられたという。また、古くから安産の守神として祀られてきたことが考えられる。現在も安産の守護として、人々に親しまれている。
神社近くの小高い丘には、干潟を望むかのように山部赤人の有名な歌碑<ref>平成24年6月に、歌碑とそれに隣接する、樹齢数百年の大木「和合の松」が倒壊した（「「和合の松」シロアリ被害で倒木」MSN産経ニュース、平成24年6月24日掲載）。現在、老松の再生を望む声もある。</ref>が建っている。近くには家庭円満の守り神として多くの人の信仰を集めてきた樹齢数百年の大木「和合の松」があったが、平成24年6月に倒壊した。名勝・和歌の浦のシンボルでもあった姿を復活させようと、通常難しいとされる挿し木によるクローン苗を使って、2018年4月に元の場所に植樹された。<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竈神社「和合の松」2018年4月19日|accessdate=2018年9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>
== 祭礼 == * [[9月16日]] - 例祭
== 文化財 == * [[和歌浦|和歌山県指定文化財 名勝・史跡和歌の浦]]
== 交通 == * [[和歌山バス]]『玉津島神社前』下車すぐ
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />

{{神社 |名称=塩竈神社 |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]][[和歌浦]]中3丁目4-25 |位置 = {{Coord|34|11|13.5|N|135|10|22.2|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=塩竈神社}} |祭神 = [[シオツチノオジ|塩槌翁尊]] |社格 = 不詳 |創建 = [[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年） |画像 = [[File:Shiogama-jinja Wakanoura Wakayama Japan02s3.jpg|220px]] |例祭 = [[9月16日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture|label=塩竈神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''塩竈神社'''（しおがまじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[和歌山市]][[和歌浦]]中にある[[神社]]。鹽竈神社とも書かれる。
== 歴史 == [[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）、鹽竈神社は[[玉津島神社]]（玉津嶋神社）の[[祓所]]から神社になった。海産物、安産の神として信仰されてきた神社で、[[神体]]の[[シオツチノオジ|塩槌翁尊]]は'''輿の窟'''という岩穴に鎮座する。
結晶片岩でできた鏡山の南面に位置する。岩肌は曝れた木理のような観を呈することから伽羅岩と呼ばれ、岩と松の組み合わさった風景が玉津島の原風景を今に伝える。祠は、海風により自然に形成された洞窟である。祠の中には小さな拝殿が造られている。元は玉津島神社の抜所で、輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた。輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた理由は、かつて浜降り神事の際に神輿が奉置される場所であったからである。浜降りとは、毎年9月16日に[[高野山]]の地主神である天野[[丹生都比売神社]]の神輿が、[[紀の川]]沿いをはるばる玉津嶋神社まで渡御し、翌日に[[日前神宮・國懸神宮|日前宮]]へと御行してゆく神事をいい、神輿が玉津嶋神社で一晩奉置されるところが興ノ窟であった。浜降り神事はその起源を古代にまで遡ることができると考えられるが、[[鎌倉時代]]に一時中断された時期があり、[[文保]]2年（[[1318年]]）に再開されたことが記録に残されている。その後戦国期に途絶え、近世には天野社の鳥居外から玉津嶋神社を遥拝するなどの神事になっている。ただ、この窟に対する信仰は、浜降り神事に限られたものではなく、[[江戸時代]]後期には、「しおかま」の名で信仰の対象になっていた。江戸時代の和歌山では「一に権現（[[紀州東照宮]]）、二に玉津島、三に下り松、四に塩竃よ」と歌われ、塩田の塩を焼く釜からこの名が付けられたという。また、古くから安産の守神として祀られてきたことが考えられる。現在も安産の守護として、人々に親しまれている。
神社近くの小高い丘には、干潟を望むかのように[[山部赤人]]の有名な歌碑<ref>平成24年6月に、歌碑とそれに隣接する、樹齢数百年の大木「和合の松」が倒壊した（「「和合の松」シロアリ被害で倒木」MSN産経ニュース、平成24年6月24日掲載）。現在、老松の再生を望む声もある。</ref>が建っている。近くには家庭円満の守り神として多くの人の信仰を集めてきた樹齢数百年の大木「和合の松」があったが、[[2012年]]（[[平成]]24年）6月に倒壊した。和歌の浦のシンボルでもあった姿を復活させようと、通常難しいとされる挿し木によるクローン苗を使って、2018年4月に元の場所に植樹された<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竈神社「和合の松」2018年4月19日|accessdate=2018年9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>。
当社を含めて一帯が国指定[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]の[[和歌浦]]となっている。
== 境内 == * 本殿 - 輿の窟と呼ばれる。 * 拝殿
== 祭礼 == * [[9月16日]] - 例祭
== 文化財 == === 国指定史跡・名勝 === * [[和歌浦]] 塩竃神社境内地 - 国指定[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]の和歌浦に当社の境内地が含まれている。
== 交通 == * [[南海電気鉄道|南海]][[和歌山市駅]]、[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[和歌山駅]]から[[和歌山バス]]新和歌浦方面行きに乗車し『玉津島神社前』下車すぐ。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[鹽竈神社]] * [[玉津島神社]] * [[不老橋]]
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:しおかましんしや}} [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:塩竈神社]]

== 歴史 == [[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）、鹽竈神社は[[玉津島神社]]（玉津嶋神社）の[[祓所]]から神社になった。海産物、安産の神として信仰されてきた神社で、[[神体]]の[[シオツチノオジ|塩槌翁尊]]は'''輿の窟'''という岩穴に鎮座する。
結晶片岩でできた鏡山の南面に位置する。岩肌は曝れた木理のような観を呈することから伽羅岩と呼ばれ、岩と松の組み合わさった風景が玉津島の原風景を今に伝える。祠は、海風により自然に形成された洞窟である。祠の中には小さな拝殿が造られている。元は玉津島神社の抜所で、輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた。輿ノ窟（こしのいわや）と呼ばれていた理由は、かつて浜降り神事の際に神輿が奉置される場所であったからである。浜降りとは、毎年9月16日に[[高野山]]の地主神である天野[[丹生都比売神社]]の神輿が、[[紀の川]]沿いをはるばる玉津嶋神社まで渡御し、翌日に[[日前神宮・國懸神宮|日前宮]]へと御行してゆく神事をいい、神輿が玉津嶋神社で一晩奉置されるところが興ノ窟であった。浜降り神事はその起源を古代にまで遡ることができると考えられるが、[[鎌倉時代]]に一時中断された時期があり、[[文保]]2年（[[1318年]]）に再開されたことが記録に残されている。その後戦国期に途絶え、近世には天野社の鳥居外から玉津嶋神社を遥拝するなどの神事になっている。ただ、この窟に対する信仰は、浜降り神事に限られたものではなく、[[江戸時代]]後期には、「しおかま」の名で信仰の対象になっていた。江戸時代の和歌山では「一に権現（[[紀州東照宮]]）、二に玉津島、三に下り松、四に塩竃よ」と歌われ、塩田の塩を焼く釜からこの名が付けられたという。また、古くから安産の守神として祀られてきたことが考えられる。現在も安産の守護として、人々に親しまれている。
神社近くの小高い丘には、干潟を望むかのように[[山部赤人]]の有名な歌碑<ref>平成24年6月に、歌碑とそれに隣接する、樹齢数百年の大木「和合の松」が倒壊した（「「和合の松」シロアリ被害で倒木」MSN産経ニュース、平成24年6月24日掲載）。現在、老松の再生を望む声もある。</ref>が建っている。近くには家庭円満の守り神として多くの人の信仰を集めてきた樹齢数百年の大木「和合の松」があったが、[[2012年]]（[[平成]]24年）6月に倒壊した。和歌の浦のシンボルでもあった姿を復活させようと、通常難しいとされる挿し木によるクローン苗を使って、2018年4月に元の場所に植樹された<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竈神社「和合の松」2018年4月19日|accessdate=2018年9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>。
当社を含めて一帯が国指定[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]の[[和歌浦]]となっている。
== 境内 == * 本殿 - 輿の窟と呼ばれる。 * 拝殿
== 祭礼 == * [[9月16日]] - 例祭
== 文化財 == === 国指定史跡・名勝 === * [[和歌浦]] 塩竃神社境内地 - 国指定[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]の和歌浦に当社の境内地が含まれている。
== 交通 == * [[南海電気鉄道|南海]][[和歌山市駅]]、[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[和歌山駅]]から[[和歌山バス]]新和歌浦行きに乗車し『玉津島神社前』下車すぐ。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[鹽竈神社]] * [[玉津島神社]] * [[不老橋]]

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|qq=|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.

== History ==
In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|qq=|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt=Salt Hammer Elder|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.

It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|qq=|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.

Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Nibu Tohime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|qq=|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]),
second, Tamatsushima,
third, Descending Pine,
fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|qq=|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.
[[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]

== Grounds ==
* Main Hall – called '''Palanquin Cave'''
* Worship Hall

== Festivals ==
* [[:en:September 16|September 16]] – Annual Festival

== Cultural Properties ==
=== National Historic Sites and Scenic Spots ===
* {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|qq=|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}} – The shrine grounds of Shiogama Shrine are included in the national historic site and scenic spot designated area of Wakaura.

== Transportation ==
* From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|qq=|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.

[[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]

== Grounds ==
* Main Hall – called '''Palanquin Cave'''
* Worship Hall

== Festivals ==

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|qq=|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.

== History ==
In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|qq=|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt=Salt Hammer Elder|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.

It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|qq=|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.

Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Nibu Tohime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|qq=|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]),
second, Tamatsushima,
third, Descending Pine,
fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|qq=|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.

== History ==
In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt=Salt Hammer Elder|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.

It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.

Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Nibu Tohime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]),
second, Tamatsushima,
third, Descending Pine,
fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.
[[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]

== Grounds ==
* Main Hall – called '''Palanquin Cave'''
* Worship Hall

== Festivals ==
* [[:en:September 16|September 16]] – Annual Festival

== Cultural Properties ==
=== National Historic Sites and Scenic Spots ===
* {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}} – The shrine grounds of Shiogama Shrine are included in the national historic site and scenic spot designated area of Wakaura.

== Transportation ==
* From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.

However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. „First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]),

The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.

Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Nibu Tohime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]),
second, Tamatsushima,
third, Descending Pine,
fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.

== History ==
In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt=Salt Hammer Elder|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.

It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.

Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]),
second, Tamatsushima,
third, Descending Pine,
fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.

* From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.

|Coordinates = {{Coord|34|11|13.5|N|135|10|22.2|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name='''Shiogama Shrine''' (塩竈神社)}}

|coordinates = {{Coord|34.187083333333334|N|135.17283333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi|WD=Q11174872}}
}}

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.

== History ==
In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.

It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.

Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]),
second, Tamatsushima,
third, Descending Pine,
fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.
[[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]

== Transportation ==
* From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.

== History ==
In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.

The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.

Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]),
second, Tamatsushima,
third, Descending Pine,
fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.

== History ==
In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.

It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.

Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]),
second, Tamatsushima,
third, Descending Pine,
fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.
[[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]

== Grounds ==
* Main Hall – called '''Palanquin Cave'''
* Worship Hall

== Festivals ==
* [[:en:September 16|September 16]] – Annual Festival

== Cultural Properties ==
=== National Historic Sites and Scenic Spots ===
* {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}} – The shrine grounds of Shiogama Shrine are included in the national historic site and scenic spot designated area of Wakaura.

== Transportation ==
* From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.

fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.
[[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.

== History ==
In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.

It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.

Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]),
second, Tamatsushima,
third, Descending Pine,
fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiogama-jinja (Wakayama)]][[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11428858]] Wikidata name: Shiogama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Shiogama Shrine''' (塩竈神社) |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Location = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}中3丁目4-25 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|11|13.5|N|135|10|22.2|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name='''Shiogama Shrine''' (塩竈神社)}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt=Salt Hammer Elder|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Type = Unknown |Founded = [[:en:1917|1917]]（[[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]6年） |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = File:Shiogama-jinja Wakanoura Wakayama Japan02s3.jpg |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Festival = [[:en:September 16|September 16]] |Map = {{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture|label='''Shiogama Shrine''' (塩竈神社)|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Wakayama|WD=Q200747}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.187083333333334|N|135.17283333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi|WD=Q11174872}} }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.
== History == In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.
It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.
Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.
The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung: „First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]), second, Tamatsushima, third, Descending Pine, fourth, Shiogama.” It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.
On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>
The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. [[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]
== Grounds == * Main Hall – called '''Palanquin Cave''' * Worship Hall
== Festivals == * [[:en:September 16|September 16]] – Annual Festival
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites and Scenic Spots === * {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}} – The shrine grounds of Shiogama Shrine are included in the national historic site and scenic spot designated area of Wakaura.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.
== References ==
<references /> == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|塩竈神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiogama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹽竈神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133753}} * {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}} * {{ill|Furou Bridge|ja|不老橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furou Bridge|1=Furōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361846}}

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.
== History == In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.
It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.
Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.
The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung: „First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]), second, Tamatsushima, third, Descending Pine, fourth, Shiogama.” It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.
On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>
The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. [[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]
== Grounds == * Main Hall – called '''Palanquin Cave''' * Worship Hall
== Festivals == * [[:en:September 16|September 16]] – Annual Festival
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites and Scenic Spots === * {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}} – The shrine grounds of Shiogama Shrine are included in the national historic site and scenic spot designated area of Wakaura.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.
