'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.
== History == In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.
It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.
Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.
The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung: „First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]), second, Tamatsushima, third, Descending Pine, fourth, Shiogama.” It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.
On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>
The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. [[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]
== Grounds == * Main Hall – called '''Palanquin Cave''' * Worship Hall
== Festivals == * [[:en:September 16|September 16]] – Annual Festival
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites and Scenic Spots === * {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}} – The shrine grounds of Shiogama Shrine are included in the national historic site and scenic spot designated area of Wakaura.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.
== References ==

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.
== History == In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.
It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.
Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung: „First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]), second, Tamatsushima, third, Descending Pine, fourth, Shiogama.” It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.
On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>
The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. '''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.

== History ==
In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.

It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.

Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]),
second, Tamatsushima,
third, Descending Pine,
fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.
[[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.

== History ==
In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.

It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.

Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]),
second, Tamatsushima,
third, Descending Pine,
fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.

In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]),

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shiogama-jinja (Wakayama)]][[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiogama-jinja (Wakayama)]][[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11428858]] Wikidata name: Shiogama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Shiogama Shrine''' (塩竈神社) |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Location = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}中3丁目4-25 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|11|13.5|N|135|10|22.2|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name='''Shiogama Shrine''' (塩竈神社)}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt=Salt Hammer Elder|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Type = Unknown |Founded = [[:en:1917|1917]]（[[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]6年） |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = File:Shiogama-jinja Wakanoura Wakayama Japan02s3.jpg |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Festival = [[:en:September 16|September 16]] |Map = {{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture|label='''Shiogama Shrine''' (塩竈神社)|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Wakayama|WD=Q200747}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.187083333333334|N|135.17283333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi|WD=Q11174872}} }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.
== History == In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.
It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.
Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.
The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung: „First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]), second, Tamatsushima, third, Descending Pine, fourth, Shiogama.” It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.
On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>
The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. [[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]
== Grounds == * Main Hall – called '''Palanquin Cave''' * Worship Hall
== Festivals == * [[:en:September 16|September 16]] – Annual Festival
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites and Scenic Spots === * {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}} – The shrine grounds of Shiogama Shrine are included in the national historic site and scenic spot designated area of Wakaura.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.

The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.
Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.
The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung: „First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]), second, Tamatsushima, third, Descending Pine, fourth, Shiogama.” It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.
On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>
The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. [[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]

== Festivals == * [[:en:September 16|September 16]] – Annual Festival
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites and Scenic Spots === * {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}} – The shrine grounds of Shiogama Shrine are included in the national historic site and scenic spot designated area of Wakaura.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.
== History == In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.
It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.
Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.
The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung: „First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]), second, Tamatsushima, third, Descending Pine, fourth, Shiogama.” It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.
On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>
The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. == Transportation == * From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.

== Transportation == * From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shiogama-jinja (Wakayama)]][[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shiogama-jinja (Wakayama)]][[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiogama-jinja (Wakayama)]][[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11428858]] Wikidata name: Shiogama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Shiogama Shrine''' (塩竈神社) |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Location = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}中3丁目4-25 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|11|13.5|N|135|10|22.2|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name='''Shiogama Shrine''' (塩竈神社)}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt=Salt Hammer Elder|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Type = Unknown |Founded = [[:en:1917|1917]]（[[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]6年） |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = File:Shiogama-jinja Wakanoura Wakayama Japan02s3.jpg |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Festival = [[:en:September 16|September 16]] |Map = {{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture|label='''Shiogama Shrine''' (塩竈神社)|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Wakayama|WD=Q200747}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.187083333333334|N|135.17283333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi|WD=Q11174872}} }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.
== History == In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.
It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.
Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.
The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung: „First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]), second, Tamatsushima, third, Descending Pine, fourth, Shiogama.” It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.
On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>
The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. '''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiogama-jinja (Wakayama)]][[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiogama-jinja (Wakayama)]][[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiogama-jinja (Wakayama)]][[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11428858]] Wikidata name: Shiogama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Shiogama Shrine''' (塩竈神社) |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Location = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}中3丁目4-25 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|11|13.5|N|135|10|22.2|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name='''Shiogama Shrine''' (塩竈神社)}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt=Salt Hammer Elder|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Type = Unknown |Founded = [[:en:1917|1917]]（[[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]6年） |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = File:Shiogama-jinja Wakanoura Wakayama Japan02s3.jpg |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Festival = [[:en:September 16|September 16]] |Map = {{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture|label='''Shiogama Shrine''' (塩竈神社)|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Wakayama|WD=Q200747}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.187083333333334|N|135.17283333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Shiotsuchi|WD=Q11174872}} }}
'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.
== History == In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.
It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.
Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.
The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung: „First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]), second, Tamatsushima, third, Descending Pine, fourth, Shiogama.” It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.
On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>
The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. [[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]
== Grounds == * Main Hall – called '''Palanquin Cave''' * Worship Hall
== Festivals == * [[:en:September 16|September 16]] – Annual Festival
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites and Scenic Spots === * {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}} – The shrine grounds of Shiogama Shrine are included in the national historic site and scenic spot designated area of Wakaura.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in central [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama City]]{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.
== History == In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.
It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.
Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.
The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung: „First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]), second, Tamatsushima, third, Descending Pine, fourth, Shiogama.” It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.
On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>
The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.
== History == In [[:en:1917|1917]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.
It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.
Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every [[:en:September 16|September 16]], the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from [[:en:Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Shrine]] of [[:en:Mount Kōya|Koyasan]] traverses along the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kino River]] all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to [[:en:Hinokuma Shrine|Nichijingu]] as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.
The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and records show that it was resumed in [[:en:Bunpō|Bunpō]] 2 ([[:en:1318|1318]]). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung: „First, Gongen ([[:en:Kishū Tōshō-gū|Kishū Tōshō-gū]]), second, Tamatsushima, third, Descending Pine, fourth, Shiogama.” It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.
On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of [[:en:Yamabe no Akahito|Yamabe Akito]], as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June [[:en:2012|2012]] (the 24th year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ 塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>
The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]] and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. [[ja:塩竈神社 (和歌山市)]]
== Grounds == * Main Hall – called '''Palanquin Cave''' * Worship Hall
== Festivals == * [[:en:September 16|September 16]] – Annual Festival
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites and Scenic Spots === * {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}} – The shrine grounds of Shiogama Shrine are included in the national historic site and scenic spot designated area of Wakaura.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai]]’s [[:en:Wakayamashi Station|Wakayama City Station]] and from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]]’s [[:en:Wakayama Station|Wakayama Station]], take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in central {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|WD=Q200747|lt=Wakayama City}}{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.

== History ==
In {{ill|1917|en|1917|WD=Q2092|lt=1917}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}}), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Shintai}} of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.

It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.

Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every {{ill|September 16|en|September 16|WD=Q2897|lt=September 16}}, the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from {{ill|Niutsuhime Shrine|en|Niutsuhime Shrine|WD=Q705154|lt=Niutsuhime Shrine}} of {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Koyasan}} traverses along the {{ill|Kinokawa River|en|Kinokawa River|WD=Q1042624|lt=Kino River}} all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to {{ill|Hinokuma Shrine|en|Hinokuma Shrine|WD=Q888184|lt=Nichijingu}} as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, and records show that it was resumed in {{ill|Bunpō|en|Bunpō|WD=Q987788|lt=Bunpō}} 2 ({{ill|1318|en|1318|WD=Q5755|lt=1318}}). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ({{ill|Kishū Tōshō-gū|en|Kishū Tōshō-gū|WD=Q11605047|lt=Kishū Tōshō-gū}}),
second, Tamatsushima,
third, Descending Pine,
fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of {{ill|Yamabe no Akahito|en|Yamabe no Akahito|WD=Q180929|lt=Yamabe Akito}}, as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June {{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012}} (the 24th year of the {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}} and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.

* Main Hall – called '''Palanquin Cave'''
* Worship Hall

== Cultural Properties ==

== Transportation ==
* From {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|en|Nankai Electric Railway|WD=Q1188230|lt=Nankai}}’s {{ill|Wakayamashi Station|en|Wakayamashi Station|WD=Q3455721|lt=Wakayama City Station}} and from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}}’s {{ill|Wakayama Station|en|Wakayama Station|WD=Q1044107|lt=Wakayama Station}}, take the {{ill|Wakayama Bus|ja|和歌山バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus|1=Wakayama Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417290}} bound for Shin-Wakaura and alight immediately at ‘Tamatsushima Shrine Front’.

'''Shiogama Shrine''' (しおがまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in central {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|WD=Q200747|lt=Wakayama City}}{{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}. It is also written as 鹽竈神社.

== History ==
In {{ill|1917|en|1917|WD=Q2092|lt=1917}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}}), Shiogama Shrine became a shrine emerging from the {{ill|Purification Hall|ja|祓所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}. It is a shrine venerated as the deity of seafood and safe childbirth, and the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Shintai}} of {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|シオツチノオジ|lt_ja=塩槌翁尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi no Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|WD=Q11174872}} is enshrined in a rock cave known as '''Palanquin Cave'''.

It is located on the southern face of Kagami Mountain, which is composed of crystalline schist. The exposed rock surface, displaying a texture reminiscent of raw wood grain, is called Kyara Rock, and the interplay of rock and pine trees preserves the original landscape of Tamatsushima. The subsidiary shrine is a cave naturally formed by the sea breeze. Inside the cave, a small worship hall has been constructed. Originally, it served as an auxiliary space of {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and was known as “Koshi no Iwaya” (こしのいわや). The name derives from the fact that, in the past, during the beach descent ritual, the portable shrine was placed there.

Hamafuri is a ritual in which, every {{ill|September 16|en|September 16|WD=Q2897|lt=September 16}}, the portable shrine of the land deity Amano from {{ill|Niutsuhime Shrine|en|Niutsuhime Shrine|WD=Q705154|lt=Niutsuhime Shrine}} of {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Koyasan}} traverses along the {{ill|Kinokawa River|en|Kinokawa River|WD=Q1042624|lt=Kino River}} all the way to {{ill|Tamatsushima Shrine|ja|玉津島神社|lt=Tamatsushima Shrine|lt_ja=玉津嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsushima Shrine|1=Tamatsushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572702}}, and on the following day proceeds to {{ill|Hinokuma Shrine|en|Hinokuma Shrine|WD=Q888184|lt=Nichijingu}} as part of the ritual, with the location where the portable shrine was enshrined overnight at Tamatsushima Shrine being known as “Koshi no Iwaya”.

The Hamafuri ritual is believed to date back to ancient times, although it was temporarily suspended during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, and records show that it was resumed in {{ill|Bunpō|en|Bunpō|WD=Q987788|lt=Bunpō}} 2 ({{ill|1318|en|1318|WD=Q5755|lt=1318}}). Thereafter, it ceased during the Sengoku period, and in later times it evolved into a ritual in which worshippers paid homage to Tamatsushima Shrine from outside the torii of Amano Shrine. However, the faith in this cave was not limited to the Hamafuri ritual; in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was venerated under the name “Shiokama”. In Edo period Wakayama, a verse was sung:
„First, Gongen ({{ill|Kishū Tōshō-gū|en|Kishū Tōshō-gū|WD=Q11605047|lt=Kishū Tōshō-gū}}),
second, Tamatsushima,
third, Descending Pine,
fourth, Shiogama.”
It is said that this name was derived from a salt kiln used to fire salt from the salt fields. It is also believed that the site has long been enshrined as the guardian deity of safe childbirth; even today, it is cherished as a protector of safe childbirth.

On a small hill near the shrine stands a famous song monument of {{ill|Yamabe no Akahito|en|Yamabe no Akahito|WD=Q180929|lt=Yamabe Akito}}, as if overlooking the tidal flats.<ref>In June 2012, the song monument and an adjacent ancient tree—“Wagō no Matsu”, a tree several hundred years old—collapsed (see “Collapse of the ‘Wagō no Matsu’ due to termite damage”, MSN Sankei News, published on June 24, 2012). There are currently voices calling for the regeneration of the old pine.</ref> Nearby, there once stood the venerable “Wagō no Matsu”, an ancient tree that had gathered the faith of many as the guardian deity of domestic harmony, but it collapsed in June {{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012}} (the 24th year of the {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} period). In an effort to revive its form—a symbol of Wakayura—a cloned sapling produced from cuttings, a method generally considered difficult, was planted in its original location in April 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2018/04/20180419_78642.html|title=２世の苗木を植樹へ　塩竃神社「和合の松」2018th year 4月19日|accessdate=2018th year 9月3日|publisher=わかやま新報|language=ja}}</ref>

The entire area, including the shrine, is designated as part of the national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}} and {{ill|Scenic Spot|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Spot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} area of {{ill|Wakaura|ja|和歌浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaura|1=Wakaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417713}}.
